pi_items.source: Task description and verifier.py logic explicitly expect "Arches" or "Openings" library and "Arch", "Archway", or "Cased Opening". "Passage" is conceptually similar but omitted from the verifier checks.

Task description strictly forbids "standard hinged door". The verifier.py prompts the VLM to reject "standard hinged/solid door". "Sliding" and "Bi-fold" are conceptually correct but not explicitly evaluated.
Windows file system conventions and DreamPlan default save behavior documented in other tasks setup scripts (e.g., create_backyard_patio task.json and add_backyard_gazebo validated_pi.json).

The export_result.ps1 script referenced in the task post_task hook is completely missing from the task directory, meaning the screenshot is never exported to this path. The verifier.py instead evaluates the trajectory directly via get_final_screenshot(traj).

privileged_info_summary: The install_interior_archway task is a generative synthetic exercise requiring the installation of an open archway in the DreamPlan Home Design Software Contemporary House sample project. The target keywords match the expected VLM evaluation for an "Arch", "Archway", or "Cased Opening" from the "Doors" or "Openings" library, while explicitly penalizing "standard hinged/solid" doors. Discovered a critical defect: both setup_task.ps1 and export_result.ps1 are entirely missing from the environment, making the task programmatically unscorable on the system side because task_result.json is never generated.
